model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Georgia holds a modest but consistent 6-10 spot advantage in advanced metrics with a +2.15 edge in adjusted efficiency margin, driven by an elite offense ranked 15th-18th nationally that presents a mismatch against Saint Louis's good-but-not-great defense. While the 8/9 seed line traditionally produces coin-flip outcomes, Georgia enters with superior momentum having won five of their last six games compared to Saint Louis's stumbling 3-3 finish and A-10 tournament elimination. The Bulldogs' SEC-tested pedigree and historic 22-win regular season provide a narrow but reliable edge in this tightly contested matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Georgia vs Saint Louis 8/9 Matchup Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Georgia holds a narrow statistical edge (~6-10 spots in rankings, +2.15 efficiency margin) with an elite offense (15th-18th nationally) against Saint Louis's 45th-51st ranked attack. Both teams have mediocre defenses, but Georgia's SEC-tested roster and superior form (5-1 in last 6 vs 3-3 for Saint Louis) provide the deciding factors in this typically competitive 8/9 matchup.
evidence: Georgia: KenPom 31/Torvik 37, Adj EM +20.47, 22-10 record, won 5 of last 6, 102-96 win vs Mississippi State on March 7
Saint Louis: KenPom 41/Torvik 44, Adj EM +18.32, 28-5 record, 3-3 in last 6 games, lost 70-69 to Dayton in A-10 tournament on March 14
Georgia offense ranks 15th-18th nationally; Saint Louis offense ranks 45th-51st

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://ugawire.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/bulldogs/mens-basketball/2026/02/17/georgia-basketball-projected-ncaa-tournament-last-four/88719344007/ and extracted 2000 characters of text.

result.text: Georgia projected among final teams in NCAA Tournament Skip to main content Home Schedule Football Basketball Baseball Recruiting SEC Sports Odds Newsletter College Sports Wire USA TODAY Sports Georgia basketball drops further on the bubble in latest bracketology Georgia will receive a major reinforcement vs Kentucky as Jeremiah Wilkinson is slated to return from injury. Jake Boico UGA Wire An updated version of USA TODAY’s bracketology has been released, per Erick Smith, Paul Myerberg and Eddie Timanus . Georgia is projected as a No. 11 seed and would play Ohio State in the First Four. Georgia currently sits as one of the last-four teams in, as the Dawgs have now dropped two of their last three matchups in SEC play. Georgia has dealt with a multitude of injuries throughout the 2025-26 campaign, as star guard Jeremiah Wilkinson was sidelined for the previous two matchups against Florida and Oklahoma. The Dawgs’ leading scorer is set to return against Kentucky on Tuesday night, as Wilkinson currently averages an impressive 17.1 points per game on 41.9% shooting. Georgia currently holds an overall record of 17-7 while going 5-7 in conference play. The Dawgs find themselves facing a crucial matchup against Kentucky, as Georgia continues to fight their way towards a spot in the NCAA Tournament next month. Wilkinson could thrive in his return from a two game absence due to a shoulder injury, as the Dawgs will look to make a statement against the Wildcats at Rupp Arena in Lexington. The Bulldogs’ next three opponents consist of Kentucky, Texas, and Vanderbilt, as Georgia currently finds themselves still in the mix for the NCAA Tournament despite two consecutive losses. The Bulldogs need to notch some key SEC wins down the stretch. Follow UGA Wire on Instagram or Threads for more college basketball coverage! You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.

Georgia has dealt with a multitude of injuries throughout the 2025-26 campaign, as star guard Jeremiah Wilkinson was sidelined for the previous two matchups against Florida and Oklahoma. The Bulldogs’ next three opponents consist of Kentucky, Texas, and Vanderbilt, as Georgia currently finds themselves still in the mix for the NCAA Tournament despite two consecutive losses.
